chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P.NO.S-1199 of 2017 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on MA-6372/2017 2. For orders on office objection. 3. For orders on MA-2537/2017 4. For katcha peshi. 02.10.2017 Mr. Imtiaz Ali Channa, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Imtiaz Ali Mahar, State Counsel. = 1. Granted. 2. Over-ruled. 3. Granted subject to all just exceptions. 4. Learned State Counsel files statement/parawise comments of respondents No.2 and 3, which are taken on record and copies whereof have been supplied to the petitioner’s Counsel. Perusal of comments, it appears that the controversy between the parties is over family dispute. It has been further stated that the petitioner has never approached the official respondents for their legal protection, however, in the comments the official respondents have stated that the petitioner will be provided legal protection as per law in the event if the petitioner approaches them with such a request. In view of the undertaking given by the official respondents in their comments, this petition is being disposed of with direction to the private respondents No.5 to 7 namely Jagsi, Devo and Wajo, all by caste Kolhi and sons of Mansingh, not to cause any harassment to the petitioner with further direction to the official respondents to discharge their functions and duties strictly in accordance with law in a fair and reasonable manner without patronizing any group or individuals. The petitioner and the private respondents are at liberty to seek remedy of their grievances, if any, before the appropriate forum by invoking due process of law and not by taking law in their hands.

In view of the undertaking given by the official respondents in their comments, this petition is being disposed of with direction to the private respondents No.5 to 7 namely Jagsi, Devo and Wajo, all by caste Kolhi and sons of Mansingh, not to cause any harassment to the petitioner with further direction to the official respondents to discharge their functions and duties strictly in accordance with law in a fair and reasonable manner without patronizing any group or individuals. The petitioner and the private respondents are at liberty to seek remedy of their grievances, if any, before the appropriate forum by invoking due process of law and not by taking law in their hands. Since the private respondents No.5 to 7 despite notice are not in attendance, therefore, the concerned SHO is also directed to serve the copy of this order on the above named private respondents by calling them in Police Station and compliance of such service should be furnished with the Additional Registrar of this Court. JUDGE Shahid
